records.descriptionEn: Gu Yuan’s early years in southern China shaped his visual sensitivity to rural landscapes, village life, and local culture.
records.descriptionCn: 古元在中國南方的成長經歷塑造了他對鄉村景觀、地方文化與日常生活的視覺感受力。

records.descriptionEn: During his years in Yan’an, Gu Yuan developed woodcut printmaking into a socially engaged visual language connected to wartime experience and rural society.
records.descriptionCn: 延安時期，古元逐漸將木刻發展為一種與戰爭經驗及鄉村社會緊密相關的社會性視覺語言。

records.descriptionEn: Following the end of the war, Gu Yuan participated in visual documentation of land reform and social transformation in northern China.
records.descriptionCn: 戰後時期，古元參與了中國北方土地改革與社會變遷的視覺記錄。

records.themeCn: 國家藝術機構與版畫發展
records.descriptionEn: In the early years of the People’s Republic of China, Gu Yuan contributed to national art publishing, printmaking development, and visual education.
records.descriptionCn: 在新中國初期，古元參與國家藝術出版、版畫發展與視覺教育建設。

records.descriptionEn: As professor and later President of the Central Academy of Fine Arts, Gu Yuan played a major role in modern Chinese art education.
records.descriptionCn: 作為中央美術學院教授及院長，古元在中國現代美術教育中發揮了重要作用。

records.descriptionEn: In his later years, Gu Yuan increasingly reflected on memory, landscape, and his relationship to his hometown and artistic legacy.
records.descriptionCn: 晚年時期，古元更多關注記憶、風景以及故鄉與藝術遺產之間的關系。

records.themeCn: 美術館、檔案與全球傳播
records.descriptionEn: Today, Gu Yuan’s works and archives continue to be preserved through museums, research, and digital initiatives aimed at international accessibility.
